privacyPage.title: Privacy
privacyPage.intro: We take your data seriously. This document explains how VisionAir processes personal data of clients and visitors of visionair.site under the GDPR.

sections.p: The data controller is VisionAir, Warszawa, Wola, Poland. Contact: hello@visionair.site, +48 453 474 944.
Data submitted through the contact form (name, phone, email, project description) is processed solely to respond to your inquiry and prepare a commercial proposal.
The legal basis for processing is your consent (Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR) and our legitimate interest in handling potential client inquiries (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR).
We store data for no longer than 24 months from the last contact or until you withdraw consent — whichever comes first.
Data may be transferred to: our hosting provider (Vercel Inc., USA — under Standard Contractual Clauses), notification service (Telegram). We do not sell your data.

You have the right to: access your data, rectification, erasure ('right to be forgotten'), restriction of processing, portability, objection, withdrawal of consent, and to file a complaint with the Polish DPA (UODO, uodo.gov.pl).
To exercise your rights or with questions about data processing: hello@visionair.site.

sections.p: Cookies are small text files stored in your browser when you visit a website. They help the site remember your actions and preferences.
These are required for basic site functionality: remembering language choice, cookie consent, form state. The site cannot work without them. They do not require consent.
These help us understand how visitors use the site (aggregated, without personal identification). Enabled only after you give consent.
You can change your preferences at any time through your browser settings or by returning to the site and updating your selection in the cookie banner.
Session cookies are deleted when you close your browser. Persistent cookies are stored for up to 12 months.

faq.title: Answers to the questions
faq.lead: If your question isn't covered here - message us on WhatsApp, we reply within an hour during business hours.

items.q: How much does drone real estate photography in Warsaw cost?
items.a: From 900 PLN net for the Basic package (15 retouched 4K photos, 48-hour delivery) up to 3 200 PLN net for Premium (40 photos + 90-second cinematic film + 360° tour + sunset session). Fixed pricing - CTR EPWA (Warsaw Chopin controlled zone) permits, color grading and commercial licence already included. Warsaw market average sits around 800-2 500 PLN for a single residential listing. No surprise add-ons after the fact.

items.q: How much should I charge for drone photos as a photographer in Warsaw?
items.a: If you are a freelance photographer pricing your own work: in Warsaw the realistic range for residential listings is 600-1 500 PLN per session (15-25 photos), and 1 500-4 000 PLN for combined photo + 60-90 second video. Premium villas and developer marketing campaigns sit at 3 000-8 000 PLN. Day rates for commercial work: 2 200 PLN/day with DJI DJI Mini 4 Pro, 5 000 PLN/day with DJI DJI Mini 4 Pro + Zenmuse X9-4K. Charging under 500 PLN signals hobby quality and burns the local price floor.

items.q: What drone should I use for real estate photography?
items.a: For most residential listings in Warsaw the best choice is the DJI DJI Mini 4 Pro with its triple-camera Hasselblad 5.1K system: quiet enough not to annoy neighbours, manoeuvrable around balconies, image quality good enough for premium portal listings and print. For premium villas, resorts and large developments use the DJI DJI Mini 4 Pro with Zenmuse X9-4K and ProRes RAW (cinema-grade). For drone walkthrough flights through interior rooms use the DJI Avata 2 FPV (cinewhoop). Consumer-grade drones like the Mini SE are not enough for portal Premium listings - flat colour, no manual exposure control, no EASA (European Union Aviation Safety Agency) C1/C2 class certification.

items.q: Where can I fly a drone in Poland and Warsaw?
items.a: Around 80% of Warsaw lies inside CTR EPWA Chopin (Warsaw Chopin controlled zone), plus EPBC Babice (Babice general aviation zone) covering the western edge of the city. Flying without coordination with PAŻP (Polish Air Navigation Services Agency) is prohibited. Across Poland: low-density rural and forest areas with VLOS (visual line of sight) below 120 m are open by default, dense urban centres almost always need authorisation. We handle all permits ourselves: pilots are EASA-certified in A1, A2, A3 plus STS-01 and STS-02 scenarios, operator registered with ULC (Urząd Lotnictwa Cywilnego - Polish Civil Aviation Authority). Typical CTR clearance window: 3-7 days, up to 14 for NOTAM (Notice to Airmen) inside the central core.

items.q: Is drone real estate photography worth it - is there really a market?
items.a: Yes. MLS (Multiple Listing Service) industry data shows listings with aerial imagery generate +403% more inquiries than ground-only photos, sell 68% faster, and command a 2-9% price premium on residential transactions. In the Warsaw market specifically, premium portals like Otodom Premium and Sotheby's International require aerial assets for top placement. For developers, a single cinematic reel typically lifts marketing-funnel conversion by 30-50%. The market is mature: agency-grade aerial coverage is now baseline expectation in Wilanów, Konstancin, Mokotów rather than a luxury add-on.

items.q: Do you do 360° aerial panoramas, 3D tours and drone walkthroughs?
items.a: Yes. 360° aerial panoramas (interactive spheres the viewer rotates in their browser) come in the Premium package. Drone walkthrough flights through the interior - a continuous cinematic single-take through rooms on a DJI Avata - are included in Standard and Premium. Full Matterport 3D tours (with floor measurements and dollhouse view) we do on request, starting at 1 500 PLN for an apartment, 2 800-3 500 PLN for a 200-300 m² house.

items.q: Who owns the photos and videos after the shoot? What rights do I get?
items.a: Full commercial rights transfer to you on payment - this is an exclusive commercial licence buyout, written as a separate clause in the contract. You can publish the material on any portal (Otodom, OLX, Morizon, Domiporta), social media, print advertising, investor decks, agency branding, and pass it on to the next owner of the property when it sells. No royalties, no time limit, no further sign-off from us required. The only right we keep is using the footage in our own portfolio and case studies - if the property is under NDA (for example, a premium villa whose owner wants privacy), we respect that fully and do not publish.

items.q: Do you work outside Warsaw - Kraków, Wrocław, Gdańsk?
items.a: Yes, across all of Poland. Within 50 km of Warsaw (Pruszków, Piaseczno, Konstancin, Łomianki, Józefów - the metropolitan ring) there is no travel surcharge. Beyond that: 1,5 PLN per kilometre one way plus pilot accommodation if overnight. We regularly shoot in Łódź, Kraków, Wrocław, Gdańsk, Poznań and the Trójmiasto (Tri-City: Gdańsk, Sopot, Gdynia) - package rates available for developers and agencies with offices in multiple cities. Minimum package for out-of-Warsaw work: Standard (1 600 PLN).

items.q: What file formats will I receive? Are they portal-ready?
items.a: Photos: JPEG 4K (3840×2160) plus pre-resized versions tuned to each portal - 1920×1280 for Otodom (largest Polish real-estate portal), 1600×1067 for OLX and Morizon, 1080×1080 square for Instagram. Video: MP4 H.264 in 4K and 1080p, plus vertical 9:16 for Reels, Stories, TikTok and Shorts, and square 1:1 for Facebook. On request: ProRes master, RAW DNG, and EXIF geotags. File sizes fit each portal's upload limit exactly - no recompression needed.

items.q: Can you shoot in winter or at night?
items.a: Yes to both. We work down to -10 °C in winter; snow cover actually creates a distinctive architectural mood ideal for premium properties. Night flights require separate EASA certification, which we hold. The best night window is the first hour after sunset (blue hour) with the property's interior lights on - it gives the warm, inhabited look that converts on portals. Rain, dense fog and high winds we reschedule for free, typically to the next suitable day.

items.q: Can you also do a roof inspection while you're up there?
items.a: Yes - a popular bonus for house listings. faq.title: Answers to the questions
faq.lead: If your question isn't here - message us on WhatsApp, we reply within an hour during business hours.

items.q: How much does drone commercial production in Warsaw cost?
items.a: Industry standard is the day rate. Base rate: 2 200 PLN/day for DJI Mini 4 Pro with pilot and assistant for online content, social and corporate spots. 5 000 PLN/day for the extended crew with ProRes RAW and live monitoring for TVC and brand films. 12 000 PLN/day for a full cinema unit on multi-day TVC and premium campaigns. The quote already covers pre-production, storyboard, CTR EPWA (Warsaw Chopin controlled zone) permits and base first-cut edit. Multi-day projects - 10-15% discount.

items.q: What's included in a shoot day?
items.a: Up to 8 hours on location, pilot and assistant (or more depending on the package), cinema pipeline with ProRes RAW, up to 200 GB of footage, dailies that same evening. CTR EPWA permits are on our side. Licensed music for online and social is in the quote. For TVC and OOH music rights are negotiated separately with labels or composers.

items.q: What drones do you use for commercials?
items.a: Per brief. For social and online - DJI Mini 4 Pro (4K Cinematic, up to 100 fps, quiet enough for urban shoots without filing notice). For TVC, brand films and premium campaigns - DJI Mini 4 Pro with Zenmuse X9-4K recording in ProRes RAW (the same quality as Apple TVCs and BMW i campaigns). For FPV fly-throughs in tight spaces, windows, tunnels - DJI Avata 2 cinewhoop, safe for close work around talent. For multi-cam briefs we put up 2-3 drones at once with live wireless monitoring on the video village.

items.q: Do you handle pre-production: storyboard, shot list, scouting?
items.a: Yes, in every quote. Storyboard - frame-by-frame flight description: shot type, length, speed, transitions. Cleared with the agency before we go out. Shot list - shoot-day timing. Location scout - in advance, minimum one trip for shoots in non-standard places. This is the part most drone services skip - and then lose a day on set. We don't skip it.

items.q: Can you integrate with our production and DOP?
items.a: Yes, that's the default mode. We sync color science with the DOP's main camera (Arri Alexa, RED, Sony Venice) - footage drops into a single pipeline with no re-grading. Wireless monitoring goes to video village for the director and DIT. Pilot works in lock with the DOP's camera-op over intercom. On request - VFX markers on the drone for post tracking.

items.q: Can you shoot inside CTR EPWA Chopin and in central Warsaw?
items.a: Yes, about 80% of Warsaw is CTR EPWA Chopin. We handle every permit: pilots EASA A1/A2/A3 certified + STS-01/02 BVLOS (beyond visual line of sight), operator registered with ULC (Urząd Lotnictwa Cywilnego - Polish Civil Aviation Authority). Zone clearance with PAŻP (Polish Air Navigation Services Agency) - 3-7 days, NOTAM window in the central core - up to 14 days. For TVC deadlines we run accelerated, filing with the regulator ahead of the production calendar. EPBC Babice (western zone) - separate filing, also on us.

items.q: What formats do I get: TV, online, social?
items.a: Master + versions for every platform. Hero master 16:9 in 4K H.264 or ProRes for broadcast (we know Polsat/TVP/TVN spec). Online versions 1080p H.264. Reels/TikTok/Shorts - 9:16 with the right framing for the crop (not just a center crop - re-storyboarded). YouTube pre-roll - 6/15/30-second cuts with the right hook. Meta pre-roll - 6/15/30/60 seconds. Social 1:1 for Facebook and LinkedIn. All versions included in the quote, no 'pay extra for vertical'.

items.q: Who owns the rights to the material?
items.a: Depends on the brief. The default is buyout to brief: full rights on TV, online, social, OOH, in-store, no royalties, no expiry. You can use the material in any campaign, on any platform, unlimited. RAW originals stay with us (for backup), released into your pipeline on request under NDA. Single-use licences (one campaign only) are 30-40% cheaper, negotiated separately.

items.q: Do you shoot outside Warsaw - Kraków, Gdańsk, Berlin?
items.a: Yes, across Europe. Within 50 km of Warsaw (Pruszków, Piaseczno, Konstancin) - no travel surcharge. Further into Poland - 1.5 PLN/km one way plus crew accommodation. Across Europe - shot in Berlin, Prague, Budapest, Vienna for agencies with regional budgets. For BVLOS over open water or in the mountains - STS-02 with local regulator notice (each country has its own procedure, we know every European one).

items.q: Can you turn it around fast - TVC deadline in 48-72 hours?
items.a: Yes, rush mode. Standard first cut in 5 working days. Rush: first cut in 48-72 hours, 50% surcharge on day rate. Minimum required: brief locked before shoot, storyboard signed off, locations without new permits (or we filed 7 days before the shoot). In 48 hours - that's for already-shot material or an express single-location shoot.

items.q: Do you have an English-speaking crew for foreign producers?
items.a: Yes, default. Pilot and producer speak PL, EN, UA, RU. All documents are bilingual PL/EN: contract, invoice, brief, NDA. We work with producers from London, New York, Berlin, Mumbai, Los Angeles. GDPR compliant, VAT-EU invoices, reverse-charge for foreign companies. We deliver via Frame.io, not Chinese cloud services.

items.q: Will the drone be loud during the ceremony?
items.a: DJI Mini 4 Pro is sub-250 g and around 70 dB from 5 metres. During the ceremony we fly from 15-30 m away — barely audible from the ground. We never fly directly above guests during the vows.

items.q: Do you work with our videographer?
items.a: Yes, that's the default. We align on workflow, codec and framerate, then deliver raw 4K 60p D-Log M. We can invoice the video studio directly if preferred.

items.q: How long does the drone fly on a wedding?
items.a: ESSENTIAL — up to 3 hours total. FULL DAY — up to 8 hours. Not continuous air time, but a dozen 5-15 minute sequences at key moments.

items.q: Who owns the footage?
items.a: Raw files come with a full personal-use license. If we want to use the footage for our own promo, that's a separate clause in the contract.

items.q: Do you travel outside Warsaw?
items.a: Yes, across Poland. Travel within 50 km of Warsaw is included. Beyond that — 2 PLN/km or the DESTINATION package with overnight.

items.q: What if the operator falls ill the day before the wedding?
items.a: We have a second certified pilot on the team, ready to step in within 12 hours. If shooting cannot take place due to force majeure on our side - we refund 100% of the deposit the same day. In 5 years of work we have missed 0 weddings.

items.q: What insurance do you carry and what does it actually cover?
items.a: Compensa Vienna Insurance Group, UAV operator OC (third-party liability insurance) for 5 million PLN - approximately 1.1 million EUR. That is 18 times the minimum required by ULC (Urząd Lotnictwa Cywilnego, Polish Civil Aviation Authority). The policy covers injuries to guests, damage to their vehicles, the ceremony building, and the reception area. We send the PDF policy with the date of your event 7 days before the wedding - you can pass a copy to the venue if they request it.

items.q: If the weather turns bad on the day of shooting - what happens to the deposit?
items.a: If heavy rain or wind > 38 km/h is forecast 24 hours before shooting, we reschedule free of charge to the next available day from your calendar - the deposit is retained. If rescheduling is not possible (the wedding date is fixed), we fly in short windows between showers or offer a 70% refund of the deposit. The decision is always yours.

items.q: Do you carry liability covering the crowd?

faq.title: FPV and music
faq.lead: Labels and directors most often ask about codec, licensing, safety and turnaround.

items.q: Is FPV safe indoors?
items.a: Yes, with a location scan and two pilots. Indoor speeds capped at 30-50 km/h, prop guards on. Never above people without their consent.

items.q: Can you sync the drone to the artist?
items.a: Yes. FPV pilot works on the director's monitor, 2-3 takes per sequence. Editor picks the best music sync.

items.q: What about licensing and permits?
items.a: STS-02 BVLOS and Open A1, Compensa VIG up to 5 M PLN. CTR EPWA processed in 5-14 days. Night flights — included.

items.q: Director monitor available?
items.a: Yes — HDMI from the drone to the director's monitor on set. Spare goggles for the director if they want a 1:1 view with the pilot.

items.q: How long does the whole music video take?
items.a: Pre-vis: 5-10 days. Shoot: 1-3 days. Raw delivery: 5 days from the last shoot day. The full project usually wraps in 4-6 weeks.

items.q: What if the FPV pilot cannot work on the day of shooting?
items.a: The team has 2 FPV pilots, both trained on identical kit (DJI Avata 2 + 5-inch custom cinewhoop). If the lead is unavailable - the second is on shift within 12 hours. Across 4 years of music videos - 0 missed shoot days.

items.q: What format do you deliver the master in for post-production?
items.a: Standard - ProRes 422 HQ 4K 60p (10-bit, up to 220 Mbps). For premium music videos - ProRes 4444 12-bit via external recorder Atomos Ninja V. For DOPs shooting on Sony VENICE or RED - we match colour through a 3D LUT and compressor. We deliver the master via Frame.io or Send Anywhere.

items.q: What if the key pass fails across every take?
items.a: We typically shoot 3-5 takes of every flight figure. If the key shot fails for technical reasons on our side - we return to the same location within 14 days free of charge. Written into every contract as a separate clause.

items.q: What accuracy does RTK deliver on a build?
items.a: 2-3 cm horizontal, 3-5 cm vertical under Open Sky. Enough for progress reporting, spoil heap volume, deviation detection. Surveyor-grade < 1 cm — we add GCPs.

items.q: Do banks accept your reports?
items.a: Yes. Regular work with PKO BP, mBank, Pekao, Millennium. Report format aligns with tranche-release requirements, includes GPS markers, dates, qualified operator signature.

items.q: Can you measure spoil volume?
items.a: Yes — standard deliverable. We scan the heap, compare to the previous scan, deliver m³ with ± 1-3% error. Good enough for mass balance.

items.q: How does the monthly subscription work?
items.a: Pick a day (usually first Monday), we show up on our own, fly, report. Monthly VAT invoice, two investments can share one invoice — 10% discount.

items.q: What if the site is in CTR EPWA?
items.a: We process PAŻP clearance in 5-14 days. Included in the package, no separate billing. 20+ approvals in EPWA and EPBC zones.

items.q: What if the RTK flight returns an error or the control points do not match?
items.a: Within 48 hours we run a comparative control flight free of charge. If the error remains above 5 cm under Open Sky - we refund the full cost of the monthly visit. Across 3 years this has happened once (magnetic anomaly near a substation); we re-flew the next day and the report was delivered on time.

items.q: What if the drone drops an element onto the construction site or injures a worker?
items.a: An OC (third-party liability insurance) policy of 5 million PLN (approximately 1.1 million EUR) covers injury to a person, damage to a contractor's equipment (crane, loader, mobile lift), and damage to materials and finished masonry. The pilot also carries individual professional liability insurance via ULC (Urząd Lotnictwa Cywilnego, Polish Civil Aviation Authority). For sites > 50 million PLN we extend the policy to 10 million PLN for an additional 800 PLN/month.

items.q: Where are the orthophotos stored and who has access to them?
items.a: All data - on Polish servers at OVH Polska (RODO/GDPR-compliant) or on your AWS Frankfurt S3 bucket on request. Access is limited to two people on our side (pilot + post-processor), MFA is mandatory, and access logs are provided on request. After the contract ends - data is deleted at the client's choice (immediate, 90, 180 or 365 days).

items.q: Which thermal camera do you fly?
items.a: DJI Zenmuse H20T — built-in 640×512 px thermal sensor with 0.05 °C sensitivity, 23x visible-band zoom, VOx microbolometer. Annual factory recalibration.

items.q: Does UDT accept your reports?
items.a: Yes — format aligned with UDT-CERT guidelines for PV inspections. Defect classification per IEC TS 62446-3. We work with three UDT-certified audit firms.

items.q: Do we need to shut the farm down?
items.a: No — quite the opposite, inspection must happen under load. We fly above the operating panels, low altitude and speed keep it safe.

items.q: What about repairs after the inspection?
items.a: Report includes defect class (A/B/C) and recommended action. Re-inspection after repair — 50% of the first price — to verify the fix.

items.q: Across Poland or only Warsaw?
items.a: All of Poland. PV farms > 1 MW — travel included. Smaller — 2 PLN/km or bundled with nearby sites.

items.q: What if the report misses a defect that appears later?
items.a: Coverage guarantee: if within 12 months of the inspection a hot-spot is detected on the inspected area that we did not include in our report - we run a repeat inspection free of charge. Across 3 years this has happened once (a bypass diode fault hidden under a mechanical fit), we re-flew and updated the report at no extra cost.

items.q: In what formats do you deliver hot-spot markers?
items.a: PDF report with a colour IR map, RAW R-JPEG (native DJI with per-pixel temperature), CSV table of hot-spots with GPS coordinates (WGS-84 + PL-2000 on request), KML for Google Earth, GeoTIFF for GIS systems. We build the PDF in Adobe InDesign on a corporate template - we can adapt it to yours.

items.q: What if the weather prevents shooting on the scheduled day?
items.a: For PV we need irradiance >= 600 W/m² and a clear sky. 72 hours before the visit we cross-check forecasts (ICM-COSMO + ECMWF). Bad weather - we reschedule free of charge, a new date is agreed within 24 hours. Over 3 years we have rescheduled 18% of visits - no client has lost their deposit.

faq.title: Manager and operator
faq.lead: Sample report, insurance policy, operator certificates — PDF on request.

items.q: What resolution does the 23x zoom deliver?
items.a: From 30 metres a single shot is around 1-2 mm/pixel — enough to see hairline cracks in grout. Gigapixel imagery extends this across the whole object.

items.q: Can you fly inside a chimney?
items.a: Yes, with a small drone (DJI Mini or Avata) and the dust extractor turned off during the inspection. For dirty flues — camera filter cleaning between flights.

items.q: Do PINB and UDT accept your reports?
items.a: Yes. Format aligned with Polish building standards — PN-EN 1990 for structures, PN-EN 13187 for thermography. We work with certified structural engineers.

items.q: Power lines — do you de-energise?
items.a: No. We stand off safely (15 m for MV, 30 m for HV). BVLOS protocol for distribution operators (PGE, Enea, Tauron) — dedicated clearances.

items.q: Re-inspection after repair?
items.a: Yes — 50% of the first inspection price. Same flight plan, identical positions, before/after documentation. Standard for insurers.

items.q: Who writes the final report - the engineer or the drone operator?
items.a: The final report is always signed by a certified structural engineer (Uprawnienia Budowlane Bez Ograniczeń, Polish unrestricted construction design licence, general construction specialisation). The drone operator delivers the raw photographic material, and the engineer performs the classification (A/B/C/D), the defect description, and the recommendations. This is what sets us apart from 90% of drone services - they issue a drone-inspector report, which neither insurers nor UDT (Urząd Dozoru Technicznego, Office of Technical Inspection) accept.

items.q: What if there are people working on the roof or facility?
items.a: 24 hours before the inspection we send a safety briefing to everyone working on site. On the day of the inspection - a 30-minute work pause in the flight zone (shopping centre roof, power plant grounds, chimney). Propeller guards on the drone, speed limited to 8 m/s, minimum distance to people of 5 metres.

items.q: Can inspections be carried out in rain or fog?
items.a: Rain of any intensity - we do not work (the Mavic 3 Cine is not waterproof, with a risk of optics damage). Fog with visibility < 100 m - we do not work (an EASA requirement for visual line of sight, VLOS). Light drizzle at +10-20°C - a formal refusal, we reschedule with no penalty. Dense fog in November-February - a rare event in Mazowieckie, typically 2-3 times per season.
